title: MemoryMax value is inaccessible from inside the container
body:
### Issue Description

When I specify

```
[Service]
MemoryMax=1G
```

in my `/etc/containers/systemd/db.container` file the memory limit is applied to the service slice, not the libpod payload slice:

```
find system.slice/db.service/ -name memory.max | while read L; do echo -e "$L:\n\t`cat $L`"; done
system.slice/db.service/libpod-payload-1930b4ead9d2a1d1b8c8dd6d9aa5d909a107c0cb61cb1a82df76cb58f332e1b7/memory.max:
	max
system.slice/db.service/memory.max:
	1073741824
system.slice/db.service/runtime/memory.max:
	max
```

This is a problem, because the limit is invisible from inside the container. The service in the container does some memory cleanup based on the available memory, and it can't see the cgroup limit, because it's applied on the service slice, so reading the memory limit inside the container just returns `max`

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. set MemoryMax to 4GB in config
2. start service
3. run `podman exec -it podname cat /sys/fs/cgroup/memory.max`


### Describe the results you received

`max` is returned

### Describe the results you expected

`4294967296` (4GB) is returned

### podman info output

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

openSUSE tumbleweed with the included podman version:

```
podman version
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      5.0.2
API Version:  5.0.2
Go Version:   go1.21.9
Built:        Thu Apr 18 11:52:56 2024
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

### Additional information

I do have a workaround using 

```
[Container]
PodmanArgs=--memory 4G
```
This gives the expected result:

```
podman exec -it podname cat /sys/fs/cgroup/memory.max
4294967296
```

comments.body:
applying the memory limit to the systemd service works only if you also specify `--cgroups=split` to reuse the current cgroup, otherwise that limit applies to the Podman command not the container payload.

If you want a limit for the container then you should use `PodmanArgs=--memory 4G`.  It is not a workaround but the correct solution.
